author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: Crafting the lowest hanging fruit in deep space offers a 30% efficiency boost, crafting min/max offers an additional %25 on top of that. The difference between crafting all at css and min max is aprox 60% increase but it takes a tremendous amount of crafters and transportation logistics.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: Yeah I’m just referring to crafting rfr holistically. Theres a couple assets that are no brainers like food and ammo. Those can boost your efficiency quite a Lot with little crew/ logistics. But then you have things like particle accelerators that consolidate the shipping weight of items to be far more compact but require multiple transport lanes and tons of crew.

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: Yeah the richness <> fuel tradeoff issue is one we're currently aware of. For some people, it just doesn't make sense ot traverse deep space to get the higher richness assets. That said, we're kind of in a holding pattern wrt econ changes for the time being. Doesn't make sense to make changes at the current moment...

content: Hi Chris, thx for all the answers on the foundation room. My question is related. We have ships like %87 discounted from their origination prices. For example, a JJ or a BYOS Earp. So people who bought those from or around origination prices at the time are kind of in loss. Is there a scheme to take those back to their origination prices. I know that crafting these are much expensive now and even close to origination prices but still, are you considering rebalancing their stats or some other thing to make them more fit to their original prices. To give an example, Calico Scud’s racing attributes are close to JJ but is traded almost %50 over when compared to a JJ unit. Will you expect the market to correct these?

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: If you are on the computer you were using for the referral, check the local storage to see if the referral link matches your wallet.  Press F12 ->  'Applications'  -> Local Storage -> https://play.staratlas.com/ ->   And where it says "buddyLink" the value will show you what referral link was used.   In the screen shot I poseted the referral link is "ln7zdhxnwqulduad" and it shows in both the referrals profile and in my local storage.   I think your referral key in local storage will match the referral link of your labs assistant wallet.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: There's a chance we take a hybrid approach with the crew drop, wherein there are multiples, which I've simply been calling "clones" internally, of common class crew members. And the legendary and anomalous crew are true 1:1's. But, there's also a chance we see literally millions of unique crew and components go out. Which could be challenging to manage.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: For context, current crew "liaiblity" based on owned ships is approximately 2M, based on the latest numbers from econ

author: ROME | wikiwikiwik
content: This is part of the complexity that is going into the crew pack and component minting R&D. cNFTs are likely capable of resolving the individuality of all of these assets, but the outcome of this decision is likely to lead to a considerable overhaul to how the marketplace, inventory, and in-game item identification systems all work. It's one thing to distribute all of the assets. It's another thing entirely to represent those items and make trade feasible after doing so.

From a logical standpoint, how would fighter ships be used to mine asteroids? They have guns that make rock go boom, yes. How do they collect that ? And how do they control the shatter of rocks from the projectile explosion? In 0 gravity.

author: ROME | Winston [ROME]
content: Well, this has always been a tough spot for SA.  There is a lot of messaging in the past that is either no longer relevant or no longer applicable.  Would I rely on the Sage manual for anything at this point? Nope.  Would I blame people for relying on the Sage manual? Also not.   I don't think people should be expected to troll through Discord to find out updates.   But people should also be expected to do their own research when making ship purchases.  And the number 1 caveat on all purchases is that all information is subject to change.  The team will do their best to stick to what they say but the game and technology is shifting so fast that its not always possible and in fact detrimental to the game if the team was forced in completely rigid roles.   One saving grace is that even if everything changes completely, I'm confident that we are early enough there is no bad purchase. So really buy any/all things and buckle up, because we are only up from here.

author: 🅰🅲🅴🅺🅸🅽🅶MUD
content: They have been going back and forth, on the idea of possibly giving higher quality crew, versus not, for a few years.  They have not consistently said yes, nor no.  This "teased" the idea that they would possibly give increased quality crew, if a more rare/more expensive ship was bought.  FWIW, I had larger ships earlier, and several months ago, switched to smaller ships, and moved the excess currency into ATLAS and POLIS, because I can see how these currencies can go up by 50x or some variety of this, while I expect the ships to generally 5 to 10x.  Not financial advice, just how I am currently playing the game.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: I use the young people at work as some sort of test group for web3 gaming, because we get ~20 new 16 year olds every year and as a IT company we have a lot of gamers in there.  In 2022 everyone was like 'NFTs are scam, go away', but in 2023 it was totally different. They knew Dr. Disrespect is building Deadrop and most of them already hold some crypto. Recently I distributed some Shrapnel codes and the feedback was very positive. I think Shrapnel did a great thing with the extraction packs. Most of them played, even it was 2am - 8am for us.  Quick and easy onboarding is key, but I see a lot of potential in the web3 gaming space recently.

IMO not many people have good Riggs and lots of money invested in crypto. Like I feel like majority of the users on CS and those other games don’t have NFT’S. Need to use other comparisons from crypto games. Anyone know like how many users played during betas for shrapnel or Deaddrop? Feel like these would be good comparisons. Maybe even star citizen? What’s their player counts.

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: I have to agree with most of what @[Peace DAC] TGI Lizard @Deleted User & @GoldenLover | BubblePop have said above about StarPath. I do not like to use my StarPath referral link because doing so, I feel i am being dishonest to my community by not informing them about the work around. Why would a player want to buy from the team's primary market ships using my 10% discount when they can just buy from the secondary market at 30-35% off from the players taking advantage of this work around. If players will more likely buy the ship from the secondary market is the very small referral $ worth misleading my followers? Not to me. And im not sure if the greater community understands that ship prices will not rise above 70% of the primary sale price as long as StarPath in its current form is live due to this work around. ATMTA needs revenue but lets have a fair system for all.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content:
@Kr1gs tldr: ship rarity has nothing to do with any drops from the team

Marketplace reflects v3 values for combat components. All core components for all ships are in class regardless of what’s shown on marketplace.

Make/spec bonuses are applied to all in class core components and all in/out of class combat components that are shown on the marketplace. Eg. C11 commander turrets and hardpoints get a 100%? Performance bonus. 

Rarity rating in ships means nothing performance wise. Crew and components’ rarity(tier) effect stats.

Overpriced ships compared to others get higher tier/rarity drops to compensate for higher price above basic manufacturer premiums/discounts

author: Kr1gs
content:
I’ve made this comment to some others on the side - but personally I feel like we are where we were at a year ago as a community, in the specific terms of kinda waiting to see what’s next at the end of Q123. (Im referring to sentiment perspective not a development perspective. You guys have pushed out a lot over the past year)

We got EV and then there was another lul in community momentum until SAGE/Breakpoint. The major difference between last year and this year is we’re headed into a bull instead of a bear.

Over here just patiently asking, okay what’s next but maybe more importantly when? And how do we ride that wave of sentiment through the bull market?

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I’ve tempered “hyping” and sharing too much I’m personally excited about simply because we got some backlash on that. People saying we constantly teased and didn’t deliver. I’m always living in the future, and get really excited about all of the possibilities. But people interpret what I say as coming immediately. content: I'm not opposed to imposing standard shipping times/cooldowns/etc. I was advocating for that at the beginning, but received a lot of pushback internally to simplify exiting the existing gameID so people would feel like they were rugged when we released the new gameID. However, since this is a transition that we're requiring, people thought community would prefer if we simplify/expedite exiting into a new gameID.

author: PG I BULK
content: Hey! Yeah i got the question but somehow I dont understand the sense, i'll try to explain: if you play with your main account ONI and you borrow your ship to your second account MUD, that account won't be able to use those ships to go in HRZ for 2 reasons: 1) your second account must have a suffient amount of experience and a license to fly those fleets, 2) ships, with some exception, are faction limited, which means you can use an VZUS as an USTUR but the same ship cannot be used by an ONI, only if that account reached some kind of goals with USTUR faction quest. I don't know how many limitations will be implemented in  Starbased but i think you will be able to rent your ship to whoever you want but this doesnt mean that there won't be some requirement to meet to actually use those ships. I hope I was able to express myself correctly

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: Players are not going to be able to bypass transportation requirements, no matter what. Wherever a ship/fleet exists in game, whatever coordinates, is where the rental would initiate. If you have assets in ONI, and borrow a ship under a different profile in USTUR, you'll not only be subject to the leveling requirements, but also the requirements of moving any material between the two points of those characters. I don't see any exploit here.

reply.preview: Players are not going to be able to bypass transportation requirements, no matter what. Wherever a ship/fleet exists in game, whatever coordinates, is where the rental would initiate. If you have assets in ONI, and borrow a ship under a different profile in USTUR, you'll not only be subject to the leveling requirements, but also the requirements of moving any material between the two points of those characters. I don't see any exploit here.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: We are still working through these specifics with the external teams building the fleet rental program. In one scenario, only fleets will be rentable, which means they will need to be in SAGE and configured already. And the renter could not customize that fleet. If at all possible, I'd much rather see ship rentals, which would cover your scenario. In that case, the borrower/renter would need to bring it in through a CSS just like everyone else. All still TBD
